etymology.description: variant form of 茲 (semantic and phonetic roles interchange depending on context)
etymology.interpretation: The character 玆(zī) serves as a variant of 茲(zī) (zī, “this”), and they interchangeably hold semantic and phonetic roles with slight variations dependent on context. There isn't a clear standard breakdown since their components share functions.

examples.chinese: 玆处(玆處)，环境幽美(環境幽美)。
examples.english: This place is serene and beautiful.

examples.chinese: 玆事体大(玆事體大)，需要深思熟虑(需要深思熟慮)。
examples.pinyin: Zī shì tǐ dà, xūyào shēnsī shúlǜ.
examples.english: This matter is significant and requires careful consideration.

examples.chinese: 玆因(玆因)，故作此决定(故作此決定)。
examples.pinyin: Zī yīn, gù zuò cǐ juédìng.
examples.english: For this reason, I made this decision.
